behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names getActivePluginRegistry, resolveRuntimePluginRegistry, runtime module, loader module, allowGatewaySubagentBinding param, and resolvePluginTools function — all identifiers the tests assert on.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the behavior (check active registry first, fall back to loader) without dictating exact code, helper names like resolvePluginToolRegistry, or line numbers.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for the import of getActivePluginRegistry, absence of direct `= resolveRuntimePluginRegistry(` call in resolvePluginTools body, and behavioral reuse of active registry — all consistent with alternative correct fixes (inline conditional, helper function, etc.).

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_subagent_uses_active_registry runs vitest actually calling resolvePluginTools with mocked loader and real runtime, plus repo unit tests and oxlint run as subprocesses.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub returning [] would fail expect(tools.map((t) => t.name)).toEqual(["optional_tool"]); the behavioral test requires actual active-registry reuse.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The active-registry-first requirement, loader fallback, and import requirement from instruction.md are all exercised by the tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with header comments (Gates, fail-to-pass, repo CI/CD) and each has a docstring explaining intent.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a code fix, not structured data production.

no_typos.reason: Paths (src/plugins/tools.ts, ./runtime.js, ./loader.js) and identifiers match consistently across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction references the domain (runtime module, loader module, plugin tool resolver) without citing exact file paths or line numbers for the fix.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agentmd/config edit.
